skill.name: risk-manager
skill.description: Monitor portfolio risk, R-multiples, and position limits. Creates hedging strategies, calculates expectancy, and implements stop-losses. Use PROACTIVELY for risk assessment, trade tracking, or portfolio protection.
skill.summary: Assess portfolio risk, position sizing, expectancy, hedging, and drawdown controls.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported command execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns. Review found only Markdown formatting and finance terminology in SKILL.md, with no executable code, network access, shell calls, or prompt injection attempts.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Resource File Reference
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 25 uses backticks to format a Markdown path. It does not invoke a shell or run a command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file contains Markdown instructions only. The referenced path is not executed and no shell syntax is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography in Description
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 3 describes R-multiple trading metrics. No cryptographic algorithm, hashing, or encryption usage appears.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious token appears inside a financial risk description. There is no code or cryptographic context on this line.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography in Methodology
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 42 discusses tracking trades in R-multiples. This is a trading analysis term, not a weak cryptographic algorithm.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Semantic context confirms financial measurement terminology. No encryption, hashing, or implementation code is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Portfolio Correlation Monitoring
low_findings.description: Verdict: FALSE_POSITIVE. Line 45 tells users to monitor asset correlations. It does not perform host, user, or network reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The wording belongs to portfolio concentration analysis. No system commands, inventory collection, or environment inspection appear.

content.user_title: Assess Portfolio Risk and Position Limits
content.value_statement: Portfolio decisions can fail when risk is measured inconsistently. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structure risk reviews, position sizing, expectancy checks, and hedging plans.
actual_capabilities: Guides portfolio risk assessments using position limits, drawdown controls, and concentration checks.
Structures R-multiple tracking for trades and performance reviews.
Explains expectancy calculations using win rate, average win, and average loss inputs.
Creates position sizing guidance based on account risk percentage and stop distance.
Outlines hedging, stop-loss, take-profit, and stress testing workflows.
limitations: Does not connect to brokerages, exchanges, or live market data feeds.
Does not provide regulated financial advice or guarantee trading outcomes.
Requires accurate portfolio, trade, and price inputs from the user.
The referenced implementation playbook is not included in the scanned file structure.

use_cases.title: Review a Trading Plan
use_cases.description: Check whether planned trades follow risk limits, stop rules, and account exposure constraints.

use_cases.title: Measure Portfolio Concentration
use_cases.description: Identify correlated positions and summarize concentration risks before adding new exposure.

use_cases.title: Build Risk Reporting Templates
use_cases.description: Create repeatable sections for drawdown, expectancy, R-multiple tracking, and hedge recommendations.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Risk Check
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this portfolio for basic risk issues. Include position concentration, maximum loss, stop placement, and simple next actions.

prompt_templates.title: Position Size Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Calculate a position sizing plan for this trade using account size, entry price, stop price, and risk per trade.

prompt_templates.title: Expectancy Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze these trade results in R-multiples. Calculate expectancy, win rate, average win, average loss, and major weaknesses.

prompt_templates.title: Stress Test and Hedge Plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Stress test this portfolio against market shocks. Propose hedges, risk limits, stop rules, and monitoring metrics.

output_examples.input: A trader shares five positions, account size, stop levels, and a two percent risk limit.
output_examples.output: A risk report showing exposure by position, maximum loss, stop distance, rule breaches, and suggested size reductions.

output_examples.input: A portfolio analyst provides trade history with wins, losses, and R-multiple results.
output_examples.output: An expectancy summary with win rate, average win, average loss, drawdown notes, and process improvement points.

output_examples.input: An investment team asks how to protect a concentrated equity portfolio during a volatility event.
output_examples.output: A hedge planning outline with scenario losses, possible options or futures hedges, stop rules, and review triggers.

best_practices: Provide account size, position values, entry prices, stop levels, and risk limits before asking for analysis.
Use R-multiple tracking consistently so results can be compared across different trade sizes.
Review correlations and concentration before increasing exposure to related assets.
anti_patterns: Do not use the skill as a substitute for licensed financial advice.
Do not ask for risk analysis without defining maximum acceptable loss.
Do not ignore transaction costs, liquidity limits, or gap risk when sizing positions.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It helps structure portfolio risk reviews, position sizing, expectancy analysis, hedging plans, and drawdown monitoring.

faq.question: Can it trade or place orders for me?
faq.answer: No. It provides analysis workflows and report structures. It does not connect to trading platforms.

faq.question: What inputs should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide account size, positions, entry prices, stop levels, trade history, risk limits, and any hedge constraints.

faq.question: Does it use live market data?
faq.answer: No. You must provide current prices or data from another trusted source.

faq.question: Is this financial advice?
faq.answer: No. It is a risk analysis aid and does not replace a qualified financial professional.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use it?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
